data.title: Tata Communications, NMDC, JK Cement among top gainers on Nifty Midcap 150
data.description:
At 1:00 pm on Friday, a few stocks emerged as top gainers on the Nifty Midcap 150. Tata Comm was trading at Rs 1,764.00, with a gain of 1.87%. NMDC stood at Rs 70.80, up by 1.78%. J. K. Cement was at Rs 6,542.00, with a 1.73% increase. Gujarat Fluoro was trading at Rs 3,495.20, gaining 1.49%, while Dalmia Bharat was at Rs 2,221.70, up by 1.47%.

Tata Comm has shown consistent financial performance over the years. The consolidated revenue for the quarter-ending June 2025 stood at Rs 5,959.85 Crore, compared to Rs 5,633.37 Crore in June 2024. The net profit for the same quarter was Rs 183.63 Crore, a decrease from Rs 329.90 Crore in the previous year.

The company’s annual consolidated revenue has also seen growth, with Rs 23,108.59 Crore reported for the year-ending March 2025, up from Rs 20,968.82 Crore in March 2024. However, the net profit decreased from Rs 1,766.84 Crore in March 2023 to Rs 949.59 Crore in March 2024, but increased to Rs 1,583.15 Crore in March 2025.

Key financial ratios for Tata Comm as of March 2025 include a basic EPS of Rs 64.44 and a diluted EPS of Rs 64.39. The book value per share was Rs 106.01. The company’s return on net worth was 60.78%, and it reported a debt-to-equity ratio of 3.60.

The company announced several corporate actions recently. On July 17, 2025, announcements were made regarding the raising of funds through non-convertible debentures and other restructuring activities. Additionally, amendments were made to the insider trading policy.

Here is a summary of Tata Comm's financial performance:

106.01

NMDC's revenue for the quarter ending March 2025 was Rs 7,004.59 Crore, compared to Rs 6,489.31 Crore in March 2024. The net profit for the same period was Rs 1,483.18 Crore, compared to Rs 1,415.62 Crore in the previous year.

The company’s annual revenue for the year ending March 2025 was Rs 23,905.52 Crore, an increase from Rs 21,307.85 Crore in the previous year. The net profit also increased from Rs 5,572.24 Crore in March 2024 to Rs 6,538.82 Crore in March 2025.

Key financial ratios for NMDC as of March 2025 include a basic EPS of Rs 7.44 and a book value per share of Rs 33.78. The company’s return on net worth was 22.03%, and the debt-to-equity ratio was 0.13.

NMDC has announced both interim and final dividends. The most recent interim dividend was announced on March 17, 2025, at Rs 2.30 per share. The company also announced a bonus issue on November 11, 2024, with a ratio of 2:1.

Here is a summary of NMDC's financial performance:

The company’s annual revenue for the year ending March 2025 was Rs 11,879.15 Crore, an increase from Rs 11,556.00 Crore in the previous year. The net profit also increased from Rs 789.93 Crore in March 2024 to Rs 871.58 Crore in March 2025.

Key financial ratios for J. K. Cement as of March 2025 include a basic EPS of Rs 111.44 and a book value per share of Rs 788.01. The company’s return on net worth was 14.14%, and the debt-to-equity ratio was 0.97.

The company announced a final dividend of Rs 15.00 per share on May 26, 2025. The company has been consistently paying dividends.

data.title: Japan futures hit highest since May 27 on weather woes, post 5th weekly gain
data.description:
• Japanese rubber futures closed Friday at a nearly seven-week high, rising for the fifth consecutive week, on concerns that heavy rains could disrupt tapping in key Southeast Asian producing regions.

  • The Osaka Exchange (OSE) rubber contract for December delivery , added 3.9 yen, or 1.2%, to 329.1 yen ($2.21) per kg, its highest close since May 27, and logged a 3.72% gain for the week.

  • The rubber contract on the Shanghai Futures Exchange (SHFE) for September delivery rose 240 yuan, or 1.65%, to 14,810 yuan ($2,063.08) per metric ton.

  • The most active August butadiene rubber contract on the SHFE (SHBRv1) gained 200 yuan, or 1.74%, to 11,720 yuan ($1,632.63) per metric ton.

  • Top rubber producer Thailand's meteorological agency warned of heavy rain and accumulations that may cause flash floods and overflows from July 19-24.

  • Heavy rain from the ongoing tropical depression is expected to affect key rubber production areas in Southeast Asia over the next three days, which may disrupt rubber tapping operations, said broker Everbright Futures.

  • Meanwhile, China's rubber output rose 2% year-on-year in the first half of 2025, even as the country's tyre production fell 1.1% from a year earlier in June, data from the National Bureau of Statistics showed.

  • Major Japanese automaker Mazda's U.S. sales fell 18.6% in May and 6.5% in June following the implementation of 25% tariffs on automobiles.

  • The auto industry makes up about 28% of Japan's exports to the U.S.

  • Automobile sales could influence the intensity of vehicle manufacturing, which involves using rubber-made tyres.

  • The front-month rubber contract on the Singapore Exchange's SICOM platform for August delivery last traded at 170 U.S. cents per kg, up 1.1%.

($1 = 148.7200 yen)

($1 = 7.1786 Chinese yuan)

data.title: Vijaya Diagnostic Board to Consider Q1 FY26 Results on July 28, 2025
data.description:
Vijaya Diagnostic Centre Limited has announced that a meeting of its Board of Directors is scheduled for July 28, 2025, to consider and approve the standalone and consolidated unaudited financial results for the first quarter ended June 30, 2025. The trading window, which was closed on July 1, 2025, will remain closed until 48 hours after the announcement of the financial results, concluding on July 30, 2025.

Board Meeting DetailsParticulars

Details

Meeting Date

July 28, 2025Purpose

Consider and approve Q1 FY26 financial resultsFinancial Results

Standalone and Consolidated UnauditedTrading Window Closure

Closed from July 1, 2025, to July 30, 2025

Financial Performance ReviewThe Board will review the company's financial performance for the first quarter of FY26, examining key metrics in both standalone and consolidated terms. This assessment will provide insights into the company's growth trajectory and operational efficiency during the period.Operational HighlightsIn addition to financial figures, the board will likely discuss operational highlights from the first quarter. These may include the volume of diagnostic tests conducted, expansion of services, technological upgrades, and other strategic initiatives that have influenced the company's performance.Corporate MattersThe board meeting will also address other corporate matters, which may include discussions on strategic initiatives, business development, and compliance-related issues. These discussions are crucial for guiding the company's future direction and ensuring sustainable growth.Market ImpactThe announcement of the board meeting and the subsequent release of the financial results are important events for investors and market participants. The market will be closely watching the company's performance to gauge its growth potential and overall financial health.

data.title: Copper rises on upbeat US data, China demand prospects
data.description:
Copper prices rose on Friday, supported by stronger-than-expected U.S. economic data and expectations of increased buying from top consumer China after the recent price dip.

Three-month copper on the London Metal Exchange gained 0.36% to $9,701.5 per metric ton by 0700 GMT, rising 0.49% so far this week.

The most-traded copper contract on the Shanghai Futures Exchange added 0.65% to 78,410 yuan ($10,922.74) a ton, but was still 0.28% lower this week.

"The U.S. economic data has been encouraging, boosting the hope for better copper demand and lowering the chances for immediate interest rate cuts," a Beijing-based metals analyst at a futures company said.

U.S. retail sales increased 0.6% in June after an unrevised 0.9% drop in May, while the number of Americans filing new applications for unemployment benefits fell last week, pointing to steady job growth so far in July.

"LME copper stocks have been rising, mainly at its Asia warehouses as some traders may be betting on more buying by China with recent price drops, uncertain whether this will materialise and by how much," a Shanghai-based metals analyst at a futures company said.

Total copper stocks at the LME-registered warehouses (MCUSTX-TOTAL) rose 34.8% since the end of June to 122,150 tons as of Thursday, with the spikes mainly at Asian warehouses such as in Gwangyang and Taiwan.

The focus is also on looming U.S. tariffs as the August 1 deadline nears and on details of the 50% import levies on copper.

LME tin gained 0.68% to $33,240 a ton, zinc advanced 0.42% to $2,748.5, lead (CNPB3) added 0.2% to $1,977, aluminium rose 0.19% at $2,583, and nickel up 0.09% to $15,110.

SHFE tin gained the most by 1.07% to 264,540 yuan a ton, zinc jumped 1% to 22,300 yuan, nickel rose 0.73% to 120,500 yuan, and aluminium grew 0.42% to 20,510 yuan. Lead fell 0.3% to 16,820 yuan.

For the top stories in metals and other news, click

or

($1 = 7.1786 Chinese yuan)

Listing of Hepsor AS additional shares on Baltic Main List
data.description:
Nasdaq TallinnAnnouncement from the exchange

Listing of Hepsor AS additional shares on Baltic Main List

On July 18, 2025, the Management Board of Nasdaq Tallinn decided to approve theadditional listing application of Hepsor AS and to list its 57,821 additionallyissued shares on Baltic Main List.

Proceeding from the above, the additional shares of Hepsor AS will be listed onJuly 22, 2025 or on a date close to it.

Thus, altogether 3,912,522 shares of Hepsor AS (ISIN: EE3100082306) will betraded under the trading code HPR1T on or about July 22.

data.title: Insurer CFC Considers London IPO, FT Says, Citing Sources
data.description:
• Cyber insurer CFC is exploring options that could include a listing on London's stock exchange at a valuation that could top 5 billion pounds ($6.71 billion), the Financial Times reports citing unnamed sources.
  • Private equity firms EQT and Vitruvian Partners, which own CFC, have been talking to bankers about a potential sale or IPO, the FT reports.
  • CFC and EQT declined to comment to the FT while Vitruvian Partners didn't immediately reply to its request.

Full story: https://tinyurl.com/3v5c4jnk

Write to Elena Vardon at elena.vardon@wsj.com

data.title: Barclays Rises Malaysia's 2025 GDP Forecast Despite Slowing Growth Momentum — Market Talk
data.description: Barclays' economists raise Malaysia's 2025 GDP growth forecast to 3.8% from 3.0% earlier, despite expectations for the economy to lose steam in 2H. 3Q GDP growth may slip below 4% as an unfavorable base effect is set to kick in, with growth stabilizing in 4Q, say Brian Tan and Hongying Liu in a note. Advance estimates show 1H growth likely at 4.4%, they say, but note signs of fading export momentum and weaker capital goods imports in June. They expect Bank Negara to deliver another 25 bps rate cut in November, bringing the policy rate to 2.50% after assessing 3Q GDP data. However, if exports deteriorate faster than expected, the cut could come as early as September, they add. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

data.title: Chinese Stocks End the Week Higher
data.description:
The Shanghai Composite rose 0.7% to close at 3,535, while the Shenzhen Component added 0.4% to 10,914 on Friday, with mainland stocks extending gains to reach fresh multi month highs as Beijing’s crackdown on price wars boosted sentiment.

Both the Shanghai and Shenzhen indexes also advanced 0.7% and 2%, respectively, notching fourth straight weekly rise.

China’s top leaders vowed to curb aggressive price-cutting to combat deflation, with UBS expecting a broader crackdown on “involution” competition ahead.

Unlike the 2015–16 cycle led by SOEs, analysts warn this effort may face more complex coordination.

In addition, the cabinet vowed to tighten oversight of the EV sector, targeting irrational pricing and cost pressures.

However, gains were capped by news of a 93.5% U.S. anti-dumping tariff on Chinese graphite, fueling concerns over EV supply chain disruptions.

On the corporate side, top gainers included Contemporary Amperex (2.2%), Zhongji (2%), Naura (1.9%), and Jiangsu (1.8%).

data.title: Ework Group publishes Interim Report, January - June 2025: Lower business volumes, but improved gross margin
data.description:
Ework Group AB

Press release

Stockholm 18th July 2025

Our gross margin shows a positive trend thanks to our add-on services and an increased focus on profitable business, and improved during the second quarter to 4.1 percent (3.8). Sales in the second quarter totaled SEK 3,601 M (4,151) and EBIT was SEK 45 M (52), a decrease from the year-earlier period. The effects of a generally challenging market situation, primarily in the IT consulting industry – and with restraint among clients – continued to dominate the quarter. This was the experience of most of those in the industry. This impacted our net sales negatively compared with the year-earlier period, as did the effect of the previously mentioned client agreements that were actively phased out. We see good possibilities ahead for further strengthening our operating margin once the market situation has normalized and business volume increases.

Second quarter 2025 compared to second quarter 2024

  • Net sales decreased 13 percent to SEK 3,601 M (4,151).
  • The gross margin increased to 4.1 percent (3.8).
  • EBIT decreased 14 percent to SEK 45 M (52).
  • The operating margin (EBIT) was 124 bps (125).
  • The operating margin (EBIT) in relation to gross profit was 30 percent (33).
  • Profit after financial items decreased 3 percent to SEK 42 M (43).
  • Order intake fell 16 percent to SEK 4,142 M (4,924).
  • The number of professionals on assignment averaged 10,883 (12,221), a decrease of 11 percent.
  • Earnings after tax and per share after dilution amounted to SEK 1.92 (2.00), a decrease of 4 percent.

First half of 2025 compared to 2024

  • Net sales decreased 15 percent to SEK 7,111 M (8,376).
  • The gross margin increased to 4.1 percent (3.8).
  • Operating profit fell 18 percent to SEK 79 M (97).
  • The operating margin (EBIT) was 111 bps (115).
  • The operating margin (EBIT) in relation to gross profit was 27 percent (31).
  • Profit after financial items decreased 29 percent to SEK 63 M (89).
  • Order intake fell 11 percent to SEK 8,345 M (9,407).
  • The number of professionals on assignment averaged 10,867 (12,256), a decrease of 11 percent.
  • Earnings after tax and per share after dilution amounted to SEK 2.90 (4.11), a decrease of 29 percent.

The complete report is available at www.eworkgroup.com.

Today, 18th July, at 1 pm CEST, CEO Karin Schreil and CFO Johanna Estra will present the company’s interim report for the first quarter 2025. Webcast: Presentation Q2, 2025

For more information, please contact:

Johanna Estra, CFO, Ework Group

Tel: +46 70-366 76 46, email: investorrelations@eworkgroup.com

The information in this Interim Report is mandatory for publication by Ework Group AB (publ) in accordance with the EU’s Market Abuse Regulation (MAR). The information was made public between 9:30 and 10:00 (CEST) on 18 July 2025, under the authority of the CEO.

About Ework Group

Ework Group is a leading consulting and workforce partner for comprehensive talent solutions and advisory with a global talent network of over 200,000 consultants specializing in IT/digitalization, R&D, engineering, and business development. The company is a leader in Northern Europe, with around 11,000 consultants on assignment, and is continuously expanding to meet customer needs. With a broad portfolio of talent solutions and deep industry insights, we help public and private clients effectively plan, acquire, and manage their workforce, including both permanent and contingent staff. Ework Group was founded in Sweden in 2000 and has operations in Sweden, Denmark, Norway, Finland, Poland, and Slovakia, with headquarters in Stockholm. Ework Group’s shares are listed on Nasdaq Stockholm (EWRK). www.eworkgroup.com

data.title: NEXG Unit Gets Passport Chip Supply Deal Extension from Malaysia's Home Affairs Ministry
data.description:
NEXG unit Datasonic Technologies secured a six-month contract extension from Malaysia's Ministry of Home Affairs for the supply of Malaysian passport chips, according to a Thursday Malaysian bourse filing.

Shares gained 1% recently on Friday.

This marks the fifth extension, and will run from Dec. 1, 2025, to May 31, 2026, the filing said.

data.title: Aedifica-Cofinimmo Merger Faces Delay Amid Belgian Competition Review
data.description:
Aedifica said Friday the Belgian Competition Authority sought additional information as part of an ongoing review for its proposed merger with Cofinimmo .

The Belgian real estate company noted the new information request came during the pre-notification phase, and the review process could extend beyond the initial timeline.

The deal between the two Belgian real estate companies has already received approval from competition regulators in Germany and the Netherlands.

data.title: RentGuarantor to List in London AIM Next Month
data.description:
By Najat Kantouar

RentGuarantor said its shares are expected to start trading on the Alternative Investment Market on Aug. 15, aiming to reach a market capitalization of around 34 million pounds ($45.6 million).

The company that offers online rent guarantee service to tenants said Friday that around 124.7 million ordinary shares of 10 pence each would be admitted to trading on AIM.

The company plans to withdraw its ordinary shares from trading on the Apex Segment of the Aquis Stock Exchange Growth Market, with effect from 8.00 am U.K. time on Aug. 15.

Write to Najat Kantouar at najat.kantouar@wsj.com

data.title: LCID: Lucid Stock Goes on Wild Ride After Uber Pledges $300 Million for Robotaxis
data.description:
Key points:
  • Lucid makes it out of $3 range
  • Uber to build $300 million stake
  • Duo teams up for robotaxi service

Shares of the luxury EV maker shot up 50% before coming down to a 36% gain at the close. It’s a big deal, but can it keep the stock out of the $3 range?

🚖 Lucid Rips Higher on Robotaxi Dreams

  • Lucid stock surged as much as 50% Thursday before trimming gains to close up 36% — still one of its biggest one-day moves ever. The jolt came after Uber pledged a $300 investment that’s geared toward a self-driving fleet.
  • The ride-hailing company will also invest in Nuro, a privately held self-driving startup. The three-way deal breaks down like this: Lucid builds the luxury EVs, Nuro supplies the autonomous AI systems, and Uber runs the ride-hailing ops. The goal? Deploy 20,000+ robo-Lucid-mobiles over the next six years.
  • Details are light, including pricing and who exactly will own the cars (Uber or fleet partners), but one thing is clear: this is real revenue on the horizon — not just vaporware and vibes. And that’s what investors were pricing in on Thursday.

🤔 Can It Stick?

  • Lucid’s stock has lived in the sub-$3 basement for most of 2025, weighed down by heavy cash burn, lackluster deliveries, a CEO swap, and an EV market that’s gone from red-hot to realistically lukewarm.
  • The Uber deal is big — potentially equivalent to Lucid’s entire expected production for 2025, which is about 20,000 vehicles. That’s double last year’s 9,029 output.
  • Still, investors are wondering: Is this a moonshot partnership or a path to sustainable profitability? With shares still hovering near the $3 line, the excitement may need more fuel (or revenue) to keep climbing.

🤖 Robo-Ride Wars Heat Up

  • The move puts Lucid in direct competition with the likes of Tesla , (already robo-cruising around Austin), and Alphabet’s Waymo (logging 250,000 robo-rides a week). Uber itself is already partnered with Waymo, so this adds another autonomous arrow to its quiver.
  • The edge here? This Lucid-Uber-Nuro trio aims to stand out with luxury. A Lucid Air sedan starts around $70,000 — not exactly your average UberX. Investors are betting this could carve out a new premium robotaxi niche.
  • With AI powering everything from ride-hailing to revenue multiples, this curious partnership might actually be the AI-tech blend we didn’t know we needed.

data.description:
Dexter Studios Co Ltd :

data.title: Senior plc to Launch GBP40 Million Share Buyback Following Aerostructures Divestment
data.description:
Senior plc said Friday it plans to initiate a share buyback worth 40 million pounds sterling.

The buyback will commence upon the completion of the high-technology components and systems manufacturer's sale of its aerostructures business to Sullivan Street Partners in a deal valued at up to 200 million pounds on an enterprise basis.

The buyback will be funded using the transaction proceeds.

data.description:
Flask Co Ltd :

data.title: Park-Ohio Prices $350 Million Notes Offering
data.description:
Park-Ohio Holdings said late Thursday it priced a private offering of $350 million principal amount of senior secured notes due 2030 at 99.5% of par value with a yearly interest rate of 8.5%.

Net proceeds will be used to fund the redemption of $350 million outstanding 6.625% senior notes due 2027, the company said.

The offering is expected to close on July 31, subject to customary closing conditions.

data.title: Bernstein ups Sonova as fundamental drivers improve
data.description:
** Bernstein upgrades its rating on Swiss hearing aids maker Sonova to "outperform" from "market-perform"

** The broker says the 18% decline in the share price since May is inconsistent with the recent improvement in fundamental drivers

** Stock up 2.3% at 0733 GMT

** The broker notes the U.S. hearing aid market has returned to low single-digit growth in Q2, after a mid-single-digit decline in Q1

** Germany and France also had stronger market unit growth in Q2, Bernstein adds

** A improving underlying hearing aid market, better than expected pricing at U.S. Department of Veteran Affair's and a return to Costco are tailwinds, which should more than offset the additional headwinds from FX, the broker says

data.title: FTSE 100 Attempts Gains for 2nd Session
data.description:
The FTSE 100 traded higher on Friday for a second straight session, nearing the 9,000 mark, supported by strength in energy, mining, and retail stocks.

BP rose over 1.5% after announcing the sale of its US onshore wind business to LS Power, part of its $20 billion divestment plan to streamline operations and refocus on selected low-carbon assets.

Shell also gained more than 1%, while Rio Tinto climbed over 1% as iron ore prices advanced.

In retail, Burberry surged up to 5.8% after a better-than-expected trading update, with smaller sales declines and signs that its turnaround strategy is gaining traction, despite ongoing challenges in key Asian markets.

On the downside, GSK shares dropped more than 6% after its blood cancer drug Blenrep failed to gain support from a US FDA advisory panel.

Analysts flagged the risk to billions in expected revenue, with the drug seen as central to GSK’s growth targets.

The FDA's final decision is expected by July 23.

data.title: Saab Consensus Earnings Estimates Could Rise After Strong Quarter — Market Talk
data.description: Saab full-year consensus earnings estimates could rise by 2%-4% after it delivered a strong second-quarter performance, SEB analysts write. Despite orders falling 28% on year in the quarter, they still landed 5% above consensus, the bank says. Adjusted EBIT was 11% above consensus, driven by strong sales. Saab also raised its 2025 organic growth guidance to 16%-20% from 12%-16%. SEB has a hold rating and SEK490 target price. Shares rise 11% to 531 kronor. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

data.title: Burberry CFO: Situation Around Tariffs Is Highly Dinamic
